**Knightsbridge** is a residential and retail district in [central London](/wiki/Central_London "Central London"),[[2]](#cite_note-2) south of [Hyde Park](/wiki/Hyde_Park,_London "Hyde Park, London"). It is identified in the [London Plan](/wiki/London_Plan "London Plan") as one of two international retail centres in London, alongside the [West End](/wiki/West_End_of_London "West End of London").[[3]](#cite_note-London_CAZ_actitivies-3) Knightsbridge is also the name of the roadway which runs near the south side of Hyde Park from [Hyde Park Corner](/wiki/Hyde_Park_Corner "Hyde Park Corner").

Knightsbridge is an affluent district in London with a rich history. Its name has Old English origins, meaning "bridge of the young men or [retainers](/wiki/Affinity_(medieval) "Affinity (medieval)")." The area was initially divided between local authorities and has been home to several parishes. Knightsbridge is known as the home of exclusive [department stores](/wiki/Department_store "Department store") including [Harrods](/wiki/Harrods "Harrods") and [Harvey Nichols](/wiki/Harvey_Nichols "Harvey Nichols"), banks and schools catering to a global clientele including [Hill House International Junior School](/wiki/Hill_House_School "Hill House School"), as well as for renowned restaurants and high-end salons. Property prices in the district are among the highest in the world, with the most expensive apartment at One Hyde Park selling for £100 million in 2007.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Knightsbridge is located between Exhibition Road and Sloane Street, with its southern border along [Brompton Road](/wiki/Brompton_Road "Brompton Road"), Beauchamp Place, and the western section of Pont Street. The district has been a target for high-profile crimes throughout history, including the [Spaghetti House siege](/wiki/Spaghetti_House_siege "Spaghetti House siege"), the [Walton's Restaurant bombing](/wiki/Walton%27s_Restaurant_bombing "Walton's Restaurant bombing"), and the [Knightsbridge Security Deposit robbery](/wiki/Knightsbridge_Security_Deposit_robbery "Knightsbridge Security Deposit robbery"). It is served by [Knightsbridge station](/wiki/Knightsbridge_tube_station "Knightsbridge tube station") on the Piccadilly line and [Sloane Square station](/wiki/Sloane_Square_tube_station "Sloane Square tube station") on the District and Circle lines.

## Toponymy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Toponymy")]

Knightsbridge is an ancient name, spelt in a variety of ways in Saxon and Old English, such as *Cnihtebricge* (c. 1050); *Knichtebrig* (1235); *Cnichtebrugge* (13th century); and *Knyghtesbrugg* (1364). The meaning is "bridge of the young men or retainers," from the Old English *cniht* (genitive case plural –a) and *brycg*. *Cniht*, in pre-Norman days, did not have the later meaning of a warrior on horseback, but simply meant a youth. The allusion may be to a place where *cnihtas* congregated: bridges and wells seem always to have been favourite gathering places of young people, and the original bridge was where one of the old roads to the west crossed the [River Westbourne](/wiki/River_Westbourne "River Westbourne").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) However, there is possibly a more specific reference to the important *cnihtengild* ('guild of *cnihtas*') in 11th-century London and to the limits of its jurisdiction (certainly Knightsbridge was one of the limits of the commercial jurisdiction of the [City of London](/wiki/City_of_London "City of London") in the 12th century).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Westminster_Met._B_Ward_Map_1916.svg/250px-Westminster_Met._B_Ward_Map_1916.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Westminster_Met._B_Ward_Map_1916.svg)

Map showing two Knightsbridge wards of Westminster Metropolitan Borough (to the west) as they appeared in 1916

Knightsbridge was a hamlet located primarily in the parish of [St Margaret (detached)](/wiki/Westminster_St_Margaret "Westminster St Margaret") and partly in [St Martin in the Fields](/wiki/St_Martin_in_the_Fields_(parish) "St Martin in the Fields (parish)") (the part that later became [St George Hanover Square](/wiki/St_George_Hanover_Square "St George Hanover Square")). It also extended into the parishes of [Kensington](/wiki/Kensington_(parish) "Kensington (parish)") and [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea_(parish) "Chelsea (parish)"). It was therefore divided between local authorities from a very early time.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In the time of [Edward I](/wiki/Edward_I_of_England "Edward I of England"), the manor of Knightsbridge appertained to the abbey of Westminster.[[8]](#cite_note-8) It was named after a crossing of the [River Westbourne](/wiki/River_Westbourne "River Westbourne"), which is now an [underground river](/wiki/Subterranean_river "Subterranean river"). It is recorded that the citizens of London met [Matilda of England](/wiki/Matilda_of_England "Matilda of England") at the Knight's Bridge in 1141.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

From 1885 to 1887, as a result of the [opening of trade](/wiki/Japan%E2%80%93United_Kingdom_relations#History "Japan–United Kingdom relations") between [Britain](/wiki/United_Kingdom_of_Great_Britain_and_Ireland "United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland") and the [Far East](/wiki/Far_East "Far East"), Humphreys' Hall in Knightsbridge hosted [an exhibition of Japanese culture](/wiki/Japanese_Village,_Knightsbridge "Japanese Village, Knightsbridge") in a setting built to resemble a traditional Japanese village. The exhibition was very popular, with over 250,000 visitors during its early months.[[9]](#cite_note-9) Japanese artisans illustrated "the manners, customs and art-industries of their country, attired in their national and picturesque costumes. Magnificently decorated and illuminated Buddhist temple. Five o'clock tea in the Japanese tea-house. Japanese Musical and other Entertainments. Every-day Life as in Japan".[[10]](#cite_note-10) [W. S. Gilbert](/wiki/W._S._Gilbert "W. S. Gilbert") and his wife attended the exhibition, which is said to have inspired him to write *[The Mikado](/wiki/The_Mikado "The Mikado")*.[[11]](#cite_note-11) When the Mikado requests of Ko-Ko the address of his son (Nanki-Poo) after Ko-Ko tells the Mikado that Nanki-Poo has "gone abroad", Ko-Ko replies that Nanki-Poo has gone to Knightsbridge.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Geography")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Knightsbridge "Special:EditPage/Knightsbridge") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(October 2020)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Knightsbridge is east of [Exhibition Road](/wiki/Exhibition_Road "Exhibition Road") and west of [Sloane Street](/wiki/Sloane_Street "Sloane Street"). [Brompton Road](/wiki/Brompton_Road "Brompton Road"), [Beauchamp Place](/wiki/Beauchamp_Place "Beauchamp Place") and the western section of [Pont Street](/wiki/Pont_Street "Pont Street") serve roughly as its southern border together with their adjacent gardens and squares such as [Ovington Square](/wiki/Ovington_Square "Ovington Square"), [Lennox Gardens](/wiki/Lennox_Gardens,_London "Lennox Gardens, London") and [Cadogan Square](/wiki/Cadogan_Square "Cadogan Square"). South of this area, the district fades into [Chelsea](/wiki/Chelsea,_London "Chelsea, London") while [Belgravia](/wiki/Belgravia "Belgravia") lies to the east and [South Kensington](/wiki/South_Kensington "South Kensington") to the west.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Economy")]

Knightsbridge is home to many expensive shops, including the [department stores](/wiki/Department_store "Department store") [Harrods](/wiki/Harrods "Harrods") and [Harvey Nichols](/wiki/Harvey_Nichols "Harvey Nichols"), and flagship stores of many British and international fashion houses, including those of London-based shoe designers [Jimmy Choo](/wiki/Jimmy_Choo "Jimmy Choo") and [Manolo Blahnik](/wiki/Manolo_Blahnik "Manolo Blahnik"), and two [Prada](/wiki/Prada "Prada") stores. The district also has banks that cater to wealthy individuals. Some of London's most renowned restaurants are here, as well as many exclusive hair and beauty salons, antiques and antiquities dealers, and chic bars and clubs. One of [Bonhams](/wiki/Bonhams "Bonhams") auction houses is located in Knightsbridge.[[13]](#cite_note-Bonhams_Lot_116-13)

### Property

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Property")]

The district and the road itself, which is the only definitive place within it, is small, which assists its cachet: more than half of the zone closest to its tube station (and nearer to no others) is [Knightsbridge Underground station](/wiki/Knightsbridge_tube_station "Knightsbridge tube station"). Knightsbridge had in its park side, east and west gold-coloured blocks of exceptional wealth in [philanthropist](/wiki/Philanthropist "Philanthropist") [Charles Booth](/wiki/Charles_Booth_(philanthropist) "Charles Booth (philanthropist)")'s late Victorian [Poverty Map](/wiki/Life_and_Labour_of_the_People_in_London "Life and Labour of the People in London"), formerly excluding Brompton Road to the west but extending well into Piccadilly, [St James's](/wiki/St_James%27s "St James's") to the east.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Knightsbridge is home to many of the world's richest people and has some of the highest property prices in the world. In 2014 a terrace of 427 m2 sold for £15,950,000, a home in [Montpelier Square](/wiki/Montpelier_Square "Montpelier Square").[[15]](#cite_note-15) The average asking price for all the properties in slightly wider [SW7](/wiki/SW_postcode_area "SW postcode area") was £4,348,911 (as at Autumn 2014). On-street parking spaces have sold for as much as £300,000 for a 94-year lease.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Fourteen of Britain's two hundred most expensive streets are in the neighbourhood, as defined by *The Times*.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

#### One Hyde Park

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: One Hyde Park")]

In February 2007, the world's most expensive apartment at [One Hyde Park](/wiki/One_Hyde_Park "One Hyde Park"), sold off plan for £100 million, bought by a [Qatari](/wiki/Qatar "Qatar") prince, and another apartment at the same place in February 2009, at almost the same price, was bought by a Qatari prince.[[17]](#cite_note-17) Apartments of this secure, optimum specification, address equate to in excess of £4,000 per square foot (£43,000 per square metre).

In 2014, a 16,000-square-foot (1,500-square-metre) two-storey penthouse in One Hyde Park sold for £140 million.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

#### History of property construction

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: History of property construction")]

Land in Knightsbridge is for the most part identified by City of Westminster (and by the [RBKC](/wiki/Royal_Borough_of_Kensington_and_Chelsea "Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea"), where former [Brompton](/wiki/Brompton,_London "Brompton, London") parts are included) as strengthened [planning law](/wiki/Planning_restrictions "Planning restrictions")-governed [Conservation Areas](/wiki/Conservation_Areas "Conservation Areas"): 'Albert Gate', 'Belgravia', 'Knightsbridge' and 'Knightsbridge Green'.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Properties must be offered here by developers as refurbished flats or houses meeting the enhanced architectural demands in the local Conservation Areas policy of the Local Plan. Within each many buildings are covered by the similar but separate requirements of being [listed](/wiki/Listed_building "Listed building"). Growing demand has since 2000 persuaded the authority to revise its planning policies to permit roof terraces and basement extensions, for residential facilities from leisure suites to private nightclubs, a degree of [economic liberalisation](/wiki/Liberal_economy "Liberal economy") documented by a non-tabloid paper in 2008.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

The [underlying landowners](/wiki/Reversion_(law) "Reversion (law)") of the few streets making up, without any dispute, Knightsbridge are the [Duke of Westminster](/wiki/Duke_of_Westminster "Duke of Westminster"), [Lord Cadogan](/wiki/Earl_Cadogan "Earl Cadogan") and the [Wellcome Trust](/wiki/Wellcome_Trust "Wellcome Trust") with a minority of the [freeholds](/wiki/Freehold_(law) "Freehold (law)") to houses in each street [sold to others](/wiki/Leasehold_enfranchisement "Leasehold enfranchisement"). Red-brick [Queen Anne revival](/wiki/Queen_Anne_style_architecture "Queen Anne style architecture") buildings form most of the [Cadogan Estates](/wiki/Cadogan_Estates "Cadogan Estates"), whereas white stucco-fronted houses are mostly found on the [Grosvenor Estate](/wiki/Grosvenor_Estate "Grosvenor Estate"), designed by architect [Thomas Cubitt](/wiki/Thomas_Cubitt "Thomas Cubitt").[[21]](#cite_note-21)

The [Brompton Oratory](/wiki/Brompton_Oratory "Brompton Oratory"), a place of [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") worship, marks one of the transitions into [Kensington](/wiki/Kensington "Kensington"), but [Belgravia](/wiki/Belgravia "Belgravia") and [Brompton](/wiki/Brompton,_London "Brompton, London") have competing mapped neighbourhood status in the east and south of the neighbourhood, and as they have no eponymously named tube stations or historic parish boundaries, their limits are arbitrary and the triangular salient of Brompton, administratively in Kensington, as part of [South Kensington](/wiki/South_Kensington "South Kensington"), once coloured mid-wealth by Charles Booth, is now blurred with 'Knightsbridge', into which it long projected.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Brompton is only used when the postcode and/or Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea is being emphasised, rather than the modern [Central London](/wiki/Central_London "Central London") 'district' definitions, which suggest Knightsbridge or South Kensington, either [tube station](/wiki/Knightsbridge_tube_station "Knightsbridge tube station"), being at most 350 m away and thus can be easily found on all maps.

## Crime

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Crime")]

For centuries, the area was renowned as the haunt of [highwaymen](/wiki/Highwaymen "Highwaymen"), robbers and cutthroats targeting travellers on the western route out of London, but its fortunes were transformed in the 19th century. However, the area has been a target of several high-profile crimes.

In September and October 1975, the [Spaghetti House siege](/wiki/Spaghetti_House_siege "Spaghetti House siege") happened.

In November 1975, two [civilians](/wiki/Civilian "Civilian") were killed by the [Provisional Irish Republican Army](/wiki/Provisional_Irish_Republican_Army "Provisional Irish Republican Army") (IRA) in the [Walton's Restaurant bombing](/wiki/Walton%27s_Restaurant_bombing "Walton's Restaurant bombing").

In 1980, the [Iranian Embassy siege](/wiki/Iranian_Embassy_siege "Iranian Embassy siege") took place, lasting several days. It ended when the [Special Air Service](/wiki/Special_Air_Service "Special Air Service") stormed the building, which was on live television.

In 1983, three Christmas shoppers and three [Metropolitan Police](/wiki/Metropolitan_Police "Metropolitan Police") officers were killed by an IRA [car bomb](/wiki/Car_bomb "Car bomb") [outside Harrods](/wiki/Harrods_bombing "Harrods bombing").[[22]](#cite_note-22)

In 1987, the [Knightsbridge Security Deposit centre was the target of a robbery](/wiki/Knightsbridge_Security_Deposit_robbery "Knightsbridge Security Deposit robbery") worth £60 million.

In 2005, 22-year-old [beautician](/wiki/Beautician "Beautician") Clare Bernal was gunned down by her [ex](/wiki/Ex_(relationship) "Ex (relationship)")-boyfriend Michal Pech on the shopfloor of [Harvey Nichols](/wiki/Harvey_Nichols "Harvey Nichols") in front of colleagues and shoppers before Pech fatally turned the gun on himself. The case attracted extensive coverage in the media, and Clare's mother Patricia has since led a campaign to address flaws in the system, which allowed her daughter's murder to happen.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

Many residential buildings are heavily covered by [CCTV](/wiki/CCTV "CCTV") and are staffed by security guards, and railings or bars on lower floor windows are commonplace.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Buildings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Buildings")]

To the north of the area, is the [Hyde Park Barracks](/wiki/Hyde_Park_Barracks,_London "Hyde Park Barracks, London") of the [Household Cavalry](/wiki/Household_Cavalry "Household Cavalry"), with a distinctive 33-storey tower by Sir [Basil Spence](/wiki/Basil_Spence "Basil Spence"). The [Royalty and Diplomatic Protection Department](/wiki/Royalty_and_Diplomatic_Protection_Department "Royalty and Diplomatic Protection Department") is based in [Walton Street](/wiki/Walton_Street,_London "Walton Street, London"). The [Embassy of Libya](/wiki/Embassy_of_Libya,_London "Embassy of Libya, London") is located at 15 Knightsbridge, the [Embassy of France](/wiki/Embassy_of_France,_London "Embassy of France, London") at no 58 and the [Embassy of Kuwait](/wiki/Embassy_of_Kuwait,_London "Embassy of Kuwait, London") at 2 Albert Gate, just off Knightsbridge.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

On the religious side it contains the impressive [Brompton Oratory](/wiki/Brompton_Oratory "Brompton Oratory") (Catholic church, Brompton Road) and the CoE [Holy Trinity Church](/wiki/Holy_Trinity_Brompton "Holy Trinity Brompton") behind it, a Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints in Exhibition Road, the [Russian Orthodox Church](/wiki/Dormition_Cathedral,_London "Dormition Cathedral, London") in Ennismore Gardens, [St Columba's Church of Scotland, Pont Street](/wiki/St_Columba%27s_Church,_London "St Columba's Church, London"), and [Deutsche Evangelische Christuskirche](/wiki/Deutsche_Evangelische_Christuskirche "Deutsche Evangelische Christuskirche"). The former St Saviour's church, designed by [George Basevi](/wiki/George_Basevi "George Basevi"), is now a private home.

Notably, two of the four London buildings of [Hill House School](/wiki/Hill_House_School "Hill House School") are located here at [Cadogan Gardens](/wiki/Cadogan_Gardens "Cadogan Gardens") and [Hans Place](/wiki/Hans_Place "Hans Place").

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Knightsbridge&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Transport")]

Knightsbridge takes its name from the road that runs along the south side of [Hyde Park](/wiki/Hyde_Park,_London "Hyde Park, London"), west from [Hyde Park Corner](/wiki/Hyde_Park_Corner "Hyde Park Corner"), spanning the [City of Westminster](/wiki/City_of_Westminster "City of Westminster") and the [Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea](/wiki/Royal_Borough_of_Kensington_and_Chelsea "Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea"). Up to [Brompton Road](/wiki/Brompton_Road "Brompton Road"), it is a part of the [A4 arterial road](/wiki/A4_road_(Great_Britain) "A4 road (Great Britain)"), while the remainder is part of the [A315 road](/wiki/A315_road "A315 road"). West of Rutland Gardens, the road becomes [Kensington Road](/wiki/Kensington_Road "Kensington Road").

It is served by [Knightsbridge station](/wiki/Knightsbridge_station "Knightsbridge station") on the [Piccadilly line](/wiki/Piccadilly_line "Piccadilly line"), and [Sloane Square station](/wiki/Sloane_Square_tube_station "Sloane Square tube station") on the [District](/wiki/District_line "District line") and [Circle lines](/wiki/Circle_line_(London_Underground) "Circle line (London Underground)"). [Brompton Road station](/wiki/Brompton_Road_tube_station "Brompton Road tube station") closed in 1934.

[Eric Coates](/wiki/Eric_Coates "Eric Coates") uses Knightsbridge as the setting of a march for the 3rd movement of his *[London Suite](/wiki/London_Suite_(Coates) "London Suite (Coates)")* (1933). It gained popularity through the radio show *[In Town Tonight](/wiki/In_Town_Tonight "In Town Tonight")* (1933–1960).

Knightsbridge is referenced in the [Rolling Stones](/wiki/The_Rolling_Stones "The Rolling Stones") song "[Play with Fire](/wiki/Play_with_Fire_(The_Rolling_Stones_song) "Play with Fire (The Rolling Stones song)")", released in 1965. Knightsbridge is used as an indicator of a character's affluence, as opposed to the more working class [Stepney](/wiki/Stepney "Stepney").

According to season 3 episode 'Happy New Year,' Knightsbridge is the setting of the television series "[Absolutely Fabulous](/wiki/Absolutely_Fabulous "Absolutely Fabulous")". [St. Columba's Church](/wiki/St_Columba%27s_Church,_London "St Columba's Church, London") in Knightsbridge is the setting of a wedding in the 1996 episode 'The Last Shout.'

Knightsbridge is referenced in the story book *[Neverwhere](/wiki/Neverwhere_(novel) "Neverwhere (novel)")* by [Neil Gaiman](/wiki/Neil_Gaiman "Neil Gaiman") as a place the protagonists need to cross to go to the next floating market. "The Knightsbridge" is used as metaphor for the night and its dangers they need to pass through.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

In the 2017 film *[The Foreigner](/wiki/The_Foreigner_(2017_film) "The Foreigner (2017 film)")*, Knightsbridge is the location of a detonated bomb that sets the plot in motion.

On his 2017 mixtape *Working on Dying* Swedish rapper [Bladee](/wiki/Bladee "Bladee") references Knightsbridge in the title and lyrics of a song.

Bars, clubs and restaurants of Knightsbridge frequently feature in the [Channel4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4")'s reality television show [Made in Chelsea](/wiki/Made_in_Chelsea "Made in Chelsea").
